title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
Branch Number
The Branch Number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number.
Chargeback
A Chargeback means an item that the credit union (the negotiating financial institution) deposits that is subsequently dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution.
Clearing
Clearing means the system of exchanging and reconciling payment items that result in a transfer of funds from one financial institution to another.
Clearing Replacement Document
Clearing Replacement Documents (CRDs) are official documents that meet the specifications of CPA Standard 014, Clearing Replacement Document Design Standard. CRDs can be used in place of an original paper payment item for presentment and return purposes in accordance with CPA Rule A10, Images, Image Printouts and Return Replacement Documents.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
Drawee
A Drawee is the financial institution to which a debit payment item is addressed. The Drawee is directed to pay the specified amount of money.
Drawer
A Drawer is the person who signs the cheque ordering the Drawee financial institution to pay the specified amount of money stated on it.
DREC File (BC Only)
The DREC File is a file of Canadian dollar Qualified Items drawn on the credit union or on members of the credit union. This file facilitates automatic settlement to the accounts on which the items are drawn.
DCPA File (Ontario Only)
The DCPA File is a file of Canadian dollar Qualified Items, drawn on the credit union or on members of the credit union. This file facilitates automatic settlement to the accounts on which the items are drawn.
Image File
An Image File is a file that Central 1 provides to credit unions or their service providers that contains images of cleared cheques drawn on membersâ accounts. These cheque images can be included in membersâ statements.
Incoming Clearing Item
An Incoming Clearing Item is a cheque, draft, or money order that is drawn on the credit union or member of the credit union, deposited by the Payee, and sent through the Clearing for debit to the Drawerâs account.
In-Province Item
An In-Province Item is an Incoming Clearing Item that is cleared through Vancouver, Toronto, Montreal, or Halifax.
Institution Number
The Institution Number is a 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that identifies a financial institution (e.g., 809 for BC credit unions and 828 for Ontario credit unions). The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number. The Institution Number is sometimes known as the route number.
MICR
Magnetic Ink Character Recognition (MICR) means characters printed with ink that contains particles of a magnetic material that allow for machine recognition.
Official Image
An Official Image means an image of an eligible bill created by or on behalf of a financial institution in accordance with bylaws, rules or standards made under the
Canadian Payments Act
, together with any data in relation to the eligible bill prepared in accordance with those bylaws, rules, and standards, and includes a display, a printout, a copy or any other output of that image and that data created by or on behalf of a financial institution in accordance with those bylaws, rules and standards. Each cheque that is acceptable for exchange in accordance with CPA Rule A1 or K1 is an eligible bill as defined in the
Bills of Exchange Act.
On-us Item
An On-us Item is a cheque that is deposited to an account held at the credit union and is drawn on an account held at the credit union.
ORS
The Online Return System (ORS) is a web-based system that facilitates the return of cheques. It also allows users to research deposit items and Incoming Clearing Items and to reinstate cheques. ORS also facilitates the return of AFT Transactions and the creation of AFT Notice of Change transactions.
Out-of-Province Item
An Out-of-Province Item is an Incoming Clearing Item that is cleared through Calgary or Winnipeg.
Payee
A Payee is the person to whom funds are to be paid.
QCPA File (Ontario Only)
The QCPA File is a file of US dollar Qualified Items, drawn on the credit union or on members of the credit union. This file facilitates automatic settlement to the accounts on which the items are drawn.
QREC File (BC Only)
The QREC File is a file of US dollar Qualified Items drawn on the credit union or on members of the credit union. This file facilitates automatic settlement to the accounts on which the items are drawn.
Qualified Item
Qualified Items are cheques or drafts that meet CPA standards and are MICR encoded with the Transit Number and dollar value. See CPA, Standard 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items.
Return Replacement Document (RRD)
A Return Replacement Document (RRD) is an official document that meets the specifications in CPA Standard 013, Return Replacement Document Design Standard, and which can be used for the purpose of return in accordance with CPA Rule A10, Images, Image Printouts and Return Replacement Documents.
Transit Number
The Transit Number is the Institution Number together with the Branch Number (e.g., 80912010).
Unqualified Item
Unqualified Items are cheques or drafts that donât meet CPA standards for a Qualified Item or cannot be imaged by Central 1âs imaging equipment (e.g., the item is too damaged). Central 1 determines whether an item is qualified or unqualified.
XDREC File (BC and Ontario)
The XDREC File is a file of Canadian dollar Qualified Items, drawn on the credit union or on members of the credit union. This file uses an XML message format defined by the ISO 20022 standard and facilitates automatic settlement to the accounts on which the items are drawn.
XQREC File (BC and Ontario)
The XDREC File is a file of US dollar Qualified Items, drawn on the credit union or on members of the credit union. This file uses an XML message format defined by the ISO 20022 standard and facilitates automatic settlement to the accounts on which the items are drawn.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
Qualified and Unqualified Incoming Clearing Items
Question 1
What is the difference between a Qualified Item and an Unqualified Item?
Answer
Qualified Items are cheques or drafts drawn on your credit union or a member of your credit union that can be successfully imaged, meet the CPAâs standards, and are MICR-encoded with the Transit Number and dollar value. Most of the Incoming Clearing Items are qualified and can be viewed on ORS.
Unqualified Items are cheques or drafts drawn on your credit union or a member of your credit union that cannot be successfully imaged and/or donât meet the CPAâs standards for a Qualified Item. Unqualified Items are processed separately from Qualified Items. The physical Unqualified Items and the corresponding adjustment slips are scanned to PDF and are available in ORS for download from the File and Report Exchange. See Section 4.4, Unqualified Clearing Items, for more information.
Question 2
Are Qualified and Unqualified items settled differently?
Answer
Yes. The amount provided on the CLSR report (for Canadian dollar items) and the CLUS report (for US dollar items) reflect the amount(s) Central 1 debits your credit union for Qualified Incoming Clearing Items. Central 1 also provides settlement files to your credit union or your banking system provider to facilitate settlement to your membersâ accounts.
Settlement for Unqualified Items isnât included with the Qualified Items. Instead, the total of each Unqualified/Return Cheque Adjustment Slip (which may contain multiple adjustments) is debited to your credit unionâs account. Manually debit your membersâ accounts for Unqualified Items.
Question 3
Are Qualified and Unqualified items reported differently?
Answer
Yes. Each Qualified Canadian dollar item is listed on a Central 1 clearing report:
CLI Report â Lists Canadian dollar In-Province 	Items
CLO Report â Lists Canadian dollar Out-of-	Province Items
CLUS Report â Lists US dollar items
Unqualified Items arenât included on a Central 1 clearing report. However, an electronic copy of the adjustment slip is available on the CHQ4 report and via the File and Report Exchange. See Section 4.3, Qualified US Dollar Clearing Items, for information on retrieving copies of adjustment slips via the File and Report Exchange.
Currency of Incoming Clearing Items
Question 4
Can I receive Incoming Clearing Items in foreign currencies?
Answer
Incoming Clearing Items can be received in Canadian or US dollars.
Chargebacks
Question 5
What is a Chargeback?
Answer
A Chargeback is an item deposited by your credit union that is subsequently dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution. See Section 4.5, Chargeback Items, for more information.
Question 6
Am I notified of Chargebacks?
Answer
ORS doesnât notify users of Chargebacks. Credit union staff should regularly log in to ORS and search for chargebacks as well as review the CLI, CLO, and CLUS reports.
Items Incorrectly Amount-Encoded or Cleared in the Wrong Currency
Question 7
What should I do if an incorrectly amount-encoded cheque or a cheque cleared in the wrong currency is received with my Incoming Clearing Items?
Answer
Return the cheque to the negotiating financial institution via ORS within 90 calendar days. Certification isnât permitted. The negotiating financial institution can reclear the cheque in the correct currency or with the correct amount encoded. See Section 4.6, Incorrectly Amount-Encoded Items and Cleared in Wrong Currency Items, for more information.
Viewing Incoming Clearing Items
Question 8
How do I view my Incoming Clearing Items?
Answer
Use ORS. ORS allows you to search for, view, and print Qualified Incoming Clearing Items. Physical cheques arenât returned to the credit union. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part, for more information.
END
